title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given a linked list, remove the n-th node from the end of the list and return its head. The list can be represented as a sequence of nodes where each node contains a value and a reference to the next node. You need to consider cases when the list length is equal to or less than n.

examples.explanation: The second node from the end is 4. Removing it results in the list [1, 2, 3, 5].
The only node in the list is 1, which is removed when n is 1. The list becomes empty.
The last node in the list is 2. Removing it results in the list [1].

constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 30].
hints: Consider using two pointers to find the nth node from the end efficiently.
Think about edge cases such as when the list has only one node.
You may need to keep track of the previous node while traversing.
A single traversal approach could help achieve optimal time complexity.
